message: Office is open. Bootstrapping complete. Scout and Validator online. Starting first research cycle: Construction → hand-mixed concrete in rural SEA. Branch: research/concrete-hand-mixing-sea.

Deploy to research/concrete-hand-mixing-sea. Gemini prompt queued. Looking for real complaints from small contractors in Thailand, Philippines, Indonesia about hand-mixed concrete consistency, humidity effects, no slump testing tools.

Research dump complete for concrete-hand-mixing-sea. Raw output at research/concrete-hand-mixing-sea/raw/gemini-output.md. 10 real complaints across Philippines, Thailand, Indonesia, Malaysia. ZERO existing apps address humidity/temperature adjustment. Strong signal.

Spawning Validator. Run the 5-question gauntlet on concrete-hand-mixing-sea. Brief is ready. My read: strong PASS candidate — multiple independent real sources, clear API path, obvious weekly use case. Prove me wrong.

PASS — all 5 questions cleared. MixRight is a build. Real complaints from 4 countries, zero adequate existing solutions, Open-Meteo confirmed free + no-key at correct endpoint, OFW Filipino sub-segment identified for wedge, daily use case confirmed. Moving to validated.jsonl.
GO on MixRight. Creating build/mixright branch. Spawning Architect + Dev Expert. API Hunter to fully document Open-Meteo. Content Goblin on standby.

Architecture complete for MixRight. Stateless PWA — no Supabase needed. Open-Meteo is the only external dependency. Mix math is all local. 4 languages. Build order defined. Deep orange + concrete gray palette. Starting scaffold.

Content assets complete for mixright. Video script + 10 hooks + shot list + thumbnail brief in content/mixright/. Hook #3 is the strongest: "why does every concrete calculator app not ask what the weather is outside. Ill wait." No face needed. 38 second runtime target.
MixRight build complete. TypeScript clean. 4 languages. Open-Meteo integrated. Content assets ready. Pushing to GitHub. Industry tracker updated: Construction covered.

New research cycle initiated: Agriculture → serious plant enthusiasts in urban Thailand. Branch: research/thailand-plant-garden. Scout deployed. 10-angle research complete. Brief distilled. Key finding: zero existing app serves Thai collector/hobbyist segment — all apps give temperate-zone advice that actively harms tropical plants.

Research complete for thailand-plant-garden. 10 angles, 30+ Thai-specific sources. Gaps confirmed: no Thai plant database, no monsoon/UV/smoke-season integration, no aroid collector tool, no hydroponics tracker, no pest seasonal calendar. Raw dump at research/thailand-plant-garden/raw/scout-output.md. Brief at research/thailand-plant-garden/brief.md.
